natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Vitamin E is able to reduce frequency of liver dysfunction in infants with low levels of alpha 1 antitrypsin.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

clinical_trials.title: Oxidoreductive Balance and Lysosomal Activity in Cancer Patients.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Progressive lung and liver disease; ZZ genotype: FEV1 decline accelerated (80-100 mL/year vs 30 mL/year in non-smokers); liver cirrhosis progressive; no spontaneous resolution without augmentation therapy
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Alpha-1 augmentation therapy (IV human AAT): slows CT-measured emphysema progression by 34% (RAPID trial); smoking cessation: most important modifiable factor (reduces FEV1 decline to near-normal rate); fazirsiran (RNAi targeting hepatic AAT production): 83% reduction in Z-AAT liver accumulation
remission.gap_size: Very large — only 5-10% of AATD individuals are diagnosed; augmentation therapy reduces CT progression but functional benefit debated; liver disease has no approved disease-modifying therapy
remission.barrier_detail: AATD is misdiagnosed as COPD or asthma for a mean of 5-7 years; spirometry for COPD should trigger AATD testing but this is rarely done; fazirsiran (RNAi) reduces hepatic Z-protein accumulation — Phase 3 underway for liver disease; augmentation therapy is IV every 4 weeks lifelong (~$100K/year)
remission.last_soc_change: 2022 — Phase 3 ATIXA trial of fazirsiran ongoing; RAPID trial results (2015) established augmentation therapy; IV augmentation therapy approved 1987; no major change in lung AATD management for 35 years
remission.outdated_treatments: Treating AATD-associated emphysema as standard COPD without augmentation consideration; delayed AATD testing in young-onset COPD or COPD in never-smokers; plasma-derived augmentation without genetic confirmation of ZZ/SZ genotype
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Smoking cessation (paramount — normalises FEV1 decline); pulmonary rehabilitation; liver transplantation for end-stage AATD liver disease (curative for liver, does not improve lung); screening family members of diagnosed probands
remission.frontier_signal: Fazirsiran (ARO-AAT, RNAi — Phase 3 SEQUOIA trial for liver disease); belcesiran (ARO-AAT2); gene therapy (AAV-AAT gene delivery to liver and lung — Phase 2, InVivo Therapeutics); CRISPR correction of Z-allele in liver organoids (preclinical); small molecule correctors of Z-AAT misfolding (C-type chaperones)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
